code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-APR-2020Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, mild ketonuria, and low specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal concentrating ability and possible tubular dysfunction. The urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Overall, findings are suggestive of renal dysfunction with abnormal urinary findings; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
